title: Mapping Assistant (Ariadne)
description: The Mapping Assistant uses the Ariadne service to map source terms from your raw data to OMOP standard concepts. It supports three matching strategies: verbatim (exact text match), vector (semantic embedding similarity), and LLM (large language model reasoning). You can review candidates, accept or reject mappings, and export results as CSV.
tips: Enter source terms one per line in the text area, or upload a CSV file where the first column contains the terms to map.
Use the Target Vocabulary filter to restrict results to specific vocabularies like SNOMED CT, ICD-10-CM, RxNorm, or LOINC.
Use the Target Domain filter to narrow results to Condition, Drug, Procedure, Measurement, or other OMOP domains.
After mapping, review the confidence score for each result. Scores above 80% are high confidence; lower scores need manual review.
Click any result row to expand and see all candidate matches, not just the best match, along with their confidence and match type.
Use the Accept/Reject buttons on each row to curate your mappings. Your decisions are included when you export to CSV.
The Term Cleanup section (collapsible) normalizes messy abbreviations and shorthand before mapping, improving match quality.
Export your finalized mappings as CSV using the Export CSV button for use in ETL pipelines or documentation.
